manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: server, school, hood, alien, knife, pole, figure, tube, oil.
Your opponent's team words are: shoe, triangle, microscope, fish, turkey, whale, giant, strike.
Distractor words are: octopus, revolution, agent, club, sock, smuggler, cold.
The kill words are: bat.

message.content:
.CLUE: metal
TARGETS: server, knife, pole, tube, figure, hood
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, not opponent, distractor, kill. Must ensure clue not part of any list words. Also cannot contain parts of them. So we need to pick clue that is semantically related to some of our words: server, school, hood, alien, knife, pole, figure, tube... We can choose multiple targets. Must avoid confusions with opponent words: shoe, triangle, microscope, fish, turkey, whale, giant, strike. Also avoid distractor: octopus, revolution, agent, club, sock, smuggler, cold. And kill: bat.

We want to maximize number of our words that share a clue. Perhaps "metal" relates to server (metal server?), knife (metal), pole (metal), tube (metal), figure (metal figure?), maybe not good. But could be "metal". However server can be computer server typically metal hardware. School not metal. Hood? Not metal. Alien? Could be "extraterrestrial". Not helpful.

Maybe "science" - relates to alien, tube (test tube), figure (figures), pole (magnetic pole), server (maybe not). Not bad. But "science" may also relate to microscope (opponent). That could lead to opponent word guess. So avoid.

Maybe "cooking": knife, tube (tube of ketchup?), hood (kitchen hood), server (restaurant server), school (culinary school). That's many! But "cooking" also could relate to oil (our word). Very good. However opponent words: fish, turkey are foods, could be guessed. That's dangerous. The clue "cooking" could cause opponent guess fish or turkey. Not ideal.

Perhaps "education": school, figure (educational figure), maybe "server"? Not really. hmm.

Maybe "electric": server (electric), pole (electric pole), tube (cathode ray tube), figure (electric figure? Not). Not great.

Maybe "head": hood (covers head), school (head of school?), figure (head figure?), not.

Maybe "space": alien, tube (space tube?), pole (north pole?), not.

Maybe "display": server (display server), figure (display figure), tube (cathode ray tube), pole (display pole?), not.

Could pick two words only. Simpler: clue "knife" is one of our words so can't. Could clue "cutlery" relates to knife, maybe also server (serving food). Could also relate to school (cutlery in cafeteria). Could also relate to hood (no). Might be okay.

Better clue "restaurant": server, hood (kitchen hood), oil (cooking oil), knife (cutlery), tube (soda tube?), maybe. Opponent words fish and turkey could also be menu items. Risk.

Maybe clue "metal": server (metal case), knife (metal), pole (metal), tube (metal), figure (metal figure), hood (metal hood, like car). This is many. Opponent words: fish, turkey, whale not metal. Triangle maybe shape of metal piece. microscope often metal? Could be. But less likely. However "metal" is not in any list, ok. Does it contain parts? It contains "met", not part of any word. Good.

Let's list target words: server, knife, pole, tube, figure, hood, alien? Not metal. School? Not. Oil? Not. So we can target those that are metal. That gives 5-6 words. Provide clue "metal". TARGETS: server, knife, pole, tube, figure, hood

Check for any opponent words that are metal: microscope (often metal), but opponent might guess that? Could be misled. But given clue metal could also hint microscope (made of metal). That's an opponent word, risk. Also "knife" could be metal, but also "fish" might be "metal fish" not common. Could be okay.

Maybe choose "cylindrical": tube, pole, figure (cylindrical figure), server (tower?), not great.

Alternatively clue "head": hood, school (headmaster), figure (head figure), pole (pole head?), server (head server?), alien (head alien?), not strong.

We need to ensure clue doesn't contain part of any word. "Metal" contains "me" maybe but okay.

I'll go with "metal" and target those related.

Thus output:

CLUE: metal
TARGETS: server, knife, pole, tube, figure, hood

Check no extra text
